title: Feature: per-language scorecards for mixed-language repositories
author: wang93wei

stage1.summary: Well-structured feature request for per-language score breakdowns in mixed-language repos. The maintainer has acknowledged this twice and said 'will add soon.' The current architecture already supports per-language scanning via `--lang`, and state is already language-scoped (state-{lang}.json). The main work is adding a `--by-language` flag that auto-detects languages in a repo and runs per-language scoring in a single invocation, plus aggregating the output. Not yet implemented based on code search.
conditions: Auto-detection of languages in a repo must be reliable (avoid false positives for vendored/generated code)
Per-language state files must remain independent; no new coupling between language scan states
Aggregate score computation must be clearly defined (weighted by file count? line count? equal weight?)
stage1.suggested_fix: Add a --by-language flag to scan and status commands. Implementation: detect languages present in the scan path, run per-language scoring passes, render separate score blocks in CLI output. Scorecard image generation per language is a nice-to-have follow-up. Start with CLI text output only.
